subject_information.address: 230 Tiffany Mountain Suite 863, South Michael, KY 25205

suspicious_activity.narrative: Michael Stanley, DOB 1978-07-24, an operational investment banker, conducted suspicious wire transfers totaling $98,090.16 on 2025-07-27 and multiple high-value debits totaling $71,181.08 on 2025-07-28 and 2025-07-29. These transactions, involving counterparties such as Sharp Ltd and Brown and Sons, are inconsistent with his reported annual income of $128,868. The rapid movement of funds across accounts CUST_0062_ACC_1 and CUST_0062_ACC_3 suggests potential money laundering activities. The activity occurred primarily via electronic and wire methods, raising concerns under BSA 31 CFR 1020.320 and 12 CFR 21.11.
suspicious_activity.ai_reasoning: STEP 1: Customer is an investment banker with medium risk. Significant wire transfers with high amounts not aligned with income. STEP 2: Unusual high-value transactions, rapid fund movement, multiple counterparties. STEP 3: BSA SAR filing required. STEP 4: High confidence due to volume and nature of transactions. STEP 5: Money laundering due to layering of funds.
